text:
Ging and Mito's Grandmother lives on Whale Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 1 

Appearance

Ging and Mito's Grandmother is an elderly woman with graying hair, always tied in a neat bun. She wears a matching white sweatshirt and sweatpants worn over a simple orange dress. A dirty yellow scarf is loosely wrapped around her neck. She is often seen smiling, her narrow eyes fixed.

Personality

Ging and Mito's Grandmother is cordial and honest, and seems to enjoy teasing Mito, especially about Ging.

Background

Most of what is known about her life comes from a single chapter. At some point in her life, decades before the series began, she gave birth to Ging’s father. She gave birth to Mito’s father later. Shortly before her grandson Ging turned twelve, he left to take the Hunter exam. Soon after this, Mito’s parents died in an accident while on vacation, and Ging’s father disappeared at sea. A decade later, Ging returned and asked her to temporarily care for his two-year-old son, Gon. Right as she was about to agree, Mito loudly objected and seized custody of Gon from him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 65 

Plot

Hunter Exam arc

Before Gon embarked on his journey to become a Hunter, she was enlightening Mito about how Gon has the blood of Ging in him and expects that one day he will leave his home just like other men do. 

Yorknew City arc

When Gon and Killua return to Whale Island from the Heavens Arena to visit Mito, she tells Gon about the truth about Mito's parents and Ging's father. According to her, Ging wanted her to take care of Gon for a while, but before she could say yes, Mito shouted that she would not allow Gon to be in Ging's care and later took the guardianship of Gon away from him. 

Succession Contest arc

She greets Gon when he returns to Whale Island along with Mito and is seen having dinner with them later. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 345 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

The name of Mito and Ging's Grandmother was never mentioned in the manga or either anime adaptation.

Anime and Manga Differences

The 1999 anime adaptation of Hunter × Hunter changes the familial relationships between Ging and Mito's Grandmother. Mito was changed into her eldest daughter, while Gon's mother was changed into her youngest. Ging was changed to her son-in-law, and Gon her grandson, rather than her great-grandson.

Miscellaneous

She shares her 2011 anime adaptation's Japanese voice actress with Genkai from Togashi's previous series YuYu Hakusho.
